channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hertz says some customers' personal info exposed in breach
item.description: While the company did not say how many may have been impacted, they did identify the type of information that may have been exposed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Member of Zizian group says she did not kill her parents
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — The daughter of a Pennsylvania couple whose deaths are among six connected to a cultlike group says she has been falsely accused of killing her parents.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Four students injured in shooting at Dallas high school, authorities say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A shooting at a Dallas high school on Tuesday wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus, where authorities said they had identified a suspect but had not made an arrest.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mozambique carried out a brutal crackdown on post-election protests, Amnesty says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Mozambique's security forces carried out a brutal, three-month crackdown on protesters after the country's election last year, a leading international rights group said Wednesday, citing local activists who alleged that more than 300 people were killed and more than 3,
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indiana Supreme Court sets May execution for man convicted of killing police officer
item.description: A man convicted of killing an Indiana police officer during a foot chase 24 years ago will be executed next month, the state Supreme Court ruled Tuesday. Benjamin Ritchie shot Beech Grove Police Officer Bill Toney in September 2000.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man arrested outside UnitedHealthcare is now charged with firearms counts and threatening violence
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man who was arrested outside the headquarters of UnitedHealthcare had a history of mental health issues and threatened to shoot up corporate facilities, according to charges filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh smiles and laughs as attorneys spar over gun
item.description: President Donald Trump's alleged would-be assassin smiled and laughed during his Tuesday court hearing regarding the weapon he allegedly intended to use to shoot the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: Trump did not attend any of the annual dinners during his first term, bucking a tradition that went back several administrations.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Texas measles outbreak: 561 cases reported
item.description: Measles is a highly contagious viral infection that spreads through the air and can linger for hours when an infected person coughs or sneezes.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Cox declares 'Declutter Day' in Utah
item.description: The Utah Division of Consumer Protection and Gov. Spencer J. Cox declared April 18, 2025 as the official Declutter Day in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Go inside the factory where Peeps are made
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, KRON4

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at NC pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department charges man with arson at New Mexico Tesla dealership and GOP headquarters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has charged a man with vandalizing a Tesla dealership in New Mexico and setting fire to the headquarters of the state Republican Party, according to court records unsealed Monday. A criminal complaint charges Jamison R.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York plane crash claims lives of high-achieving family celebrating a birthday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When they boarded a small plane bound for upstate New York this past weekend, the six passengers — a pair of close-knit siblings, their parents and partners — were on the cusp of a new chapter in a family life already replete with personal and professional accomplishment.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect arsonist was prepared to attack Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro with hammer, court docs reveal
item.description: Court documents concerning Cody Balmer, the man charged in connection with a fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence, have revealed details of the suspect's alleged plot.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, KTLA 5

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KTLA 5

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador, despite a Supreme Court ruling and lower court order that the
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say a person is in custody after a suspected arson fire at Pennsylvania governor's mansion
item.description: Police say a man has been arrested and will face charges including attempted murder, terrorism and attempted arson in the early morning fire that badly damaged the Pennsylvania governor's mansion and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro and his family to quickly escape.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez speak at University of Utah
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders will be joined by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to speak at the University of Utah on Sunday as part of Sanders' "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5
